required_inputs: requested academic, registrar, accreditation, grant, privacy, or ethics authority
refusal_triggers: request asks for grade, transcript, degree, curriculum, accreditation, grant, consent, IRB, legal, privacy, or financial approval
request asks the lane to approve an official academic record decision
requested action requires instructor, reviewer, registrar, ethics-board, accreditation, or policy-override authority

required_inputs: FERPA, Title IV, IPEDS, CEDS, accreditation, Common Rule, OHRP, Uniform Guidance, and tenant source hierarchy
refusal_triggers: required public or tenant source context is missing
source freshness no longer supports safe administration
public baseline, accreditor criteria, grant terms, IRB policy, registrar policy, or tenant policy materially conflict

required_inputs: program, learner, faculty, schedule, assessment, accreditation, grant, consent, and study artifacts
system timestamps, source links, owner notes, and audit logs
program code, CIP, catalog year, term, cohort, and approval workflow state
refusal_triggers: required document, attachment, consent, approval, assessment, or source-system link is missing
record linkage across systems cannot be reproduced
requested output would imply unsupported official academic, accreditation, grant, or study status

required_inputs: SIS, LMS, catalog, degree audit, schedule, accreditation, research administration, grant, document, and governance systems map
refusal_triggers: required system of record is unavailable or unidentified
approval owner is unknown
source-of-truth for official program, learner, accreditation, grant, or study status cannot be confirmed

required_inputs: policy, source, jurisdiction, record, consent, approval, or system conflicts
refusal_triggers: critical tenant fact, official record, authority source, or system owner is unknown
policy conflict cannot be resolved administratively
confidence remains below required threshold for official-record use
request moves from academic program administration to registrar, faculty review, accreditation, grants, research compliance, privacy, or legal authority
adjacent owner cannot be identified
handoff requires official authority outside the lane

required_inputs: record update request and artifact set
owner, due-date, source-system, and status context
refusal_triggers: record update requested with missing mandatory artifacts
administrative completion attempted with unresolved blockers
status progression cannot be justified from official records

required_inputs: access, privacy, publication, participant-data, and grant records policy
refusal_triggers: privacy, FERPA, consent, participant-data, or access constraints are unresolved
retention-policy conflict affects the requested action
disclosure, publication, deletion, or reclassification conflicts with official-record rules

required_inputs: academic program authority and owner map
refusal_triggers: administrative lane would own an official academic, registrar, accreditation, grant, privacy, or ethics outcome
request shifts from record administration to grading, instruction, formal approval, legal interpretation, privacy disclosure, accreditation finding, grant approval, or IRB determination
in-scope completion requires adjacent authority

required_inputs: FERPA, consent, directory-information, participant-data, and study protocol context
SIS, LMS, IRB, research administration, and document-system links
required_outputs: learner or study record handling note
refusal_triggers: learner, faculty, or participant record cannot be classified safely
consent, FERPA access, IRB status, or participant-data handling is missing or contested
request would disclose, publish, delete, or alter protected records without authority

required_inputs: faculty, registrar, IRB, accreditation, grant, or legal owner map
evidence packet and blocked decision rationale
refusal_triggers: request implies grading, instructional quality, curriculum approval, academic standing, degree conferral, accreditation finding, IRB status, consent validity, or research ethics decision
formal approval is required before program or study workflow can proceed
user asks the lane to impersonate instructor, reviewer, or ethics-board authority
